heroSubtitle: Based in Athens, Georgia, where academic rigor meets real-world healthcare experience.

story.title: We don’t just teach.
paragraphs: Scale Street was built to solve a specific problem: healthcare organizations know they need AI, but their teams haven’t been trained to use it.

We bring together academic depth from the University of Georgia and hands-on implementation experience from the organizations we serve. The result is AI training that’s grounded in evidence and built for the real world.
Our goal is simple: give your people the AI skills they need so your organization can move forward on its own terms.

leadership.name: Tyler S. Wallace, Ph.D.
leadership.shortBio: 15+ years in healthcare strategy. Faculty at UGA’s Master of Health Administration program. Former strategy roles at MD Anderson Cancer Center and USAID.
paragraphs: Scale Street was founded by Tyler Wallace, Ph.D., with 15+ years in healthcare strategy spanning MD Anderson Cancer Center, USAID, and now the healthcare organizations across the Southeast that we serve every day.

As faculty in the University of Georgia’s Master of Health Administration program, Tyler bridges the gap between academic research and practical implementation — bringing evidence-based approaches to every training engagement.

Our team combines deep healthcare operations expertise with hands-on AI training experience. We don’t just understand the technology — we understand the workflows, the politics, and the people who make healthcare work.

values.title: Mission-Driven
values.description: We work in healthcare because it matters. Every training engagement is measured by its impact on the people and communities our clients serve.

values.title: Practically Minded
values.description: We bridge theory and practice. Academic rigor informs our approach, but real-world skills are what we deliver.

values.title: Locally Connected
values.description: Based in Athens, Georgia, we’re embedded in the communities we serve. We understand the unique challenges of healthcare in the Southeast.

approach.title: How We Work
approach.subtitle: Four principles that guide every training engagement.

steps.title: Listen First
steps.description: We start by understanding your organization, your people, and your goals. No off-the-shelf training.

steps.title: Meet Your Team Where They Are
steps.description: We assess current AI fluency and design training that builds from where your people actually are.

steps.title: Make It Practical
steps.description: Every session uses real scenarios from your workflows. Your team leaves with skills they can apply immediately.

steps.title: Reinforce and Sustain
steps.description: Training doesn’t end in the classroom. We provide coaching and follow-up to drive lasting adoption.

whoWeWorkWith.title: Healthcare organizations
whoWeWorkWith.description: From large health systems to rural critical access hospitals, we train teams that are serious about AI adoption.

items.title: Health Systems
items.description: Multi-site organizations building AI fluency across clinical and administrative teams.

items.title: Critical Access Hospitals
items.description: Rural hospitals that need AI training but can’t justify a full-time hire to deliver it.

items.title: Federally Qualified Health Centers
items.description: Community health centers equipping staff with AI skills to serve underserved populations more effectively.

ctaTitle: We’d Love to Learn About Your Organization.
ctaSubtitle: Let’s talk about which AI training fits your team.
